description: §48.3.1 clean neg — the SAME `?{}` SQL access as `fn/sql-access-reject`, but inside a plain `function` (not a `fn`) body, SHALL NOT fire E-FN-001. The purity prohibition is `fn`-scoped; a `function` may perform database operations (it route-infers to the server per §12.2). `notCodes` asserts E-FN-001 stays silent.
rationale: §48.3.1 is a `fn`-body prohibition — it does not apply to `function` bodies. A `?{}` query in a `function` is the normal server-fn shape (§12.2 route inference), not E-FN-001.
